purpose: Persistent registry of tool cards that already received editorial mid-article illustrations. Check this file before adding future tool-card artwork so the same page is not illustrated twice.
selection_rule: Exclude every slug listed in batches[].slugs unless the user explicitly asks to replace or revise an existing illustration.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features section
batches.note: Second generation run only. The first stopped run was discarded because it looked too uniform; this batch uses one distinct tool-specific visual metaphor per tool.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure after the first substantive section
batches.note: Second production batch: starts with homepage pinned tools, then highest-popularity non-illustrated tools. Generated through the Codex image generation flow, matching the approved first batch style direction.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure after the first audience/context section
batches.note: Third production batch: next highest-popularity non-illustrated tools after the first 50 illustrated cards. Generated through the Codex image generation flow; styles and palettes deliberately vary per tool.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features section
batches.note: Fourth production batch: first covers remaining non-illustrated tools with non-zero DE/EN popularity, then adds high-intent known tools from the catalogue. The batch intentionally broadens styles across poster, collage, cinematic, clay, blueprint, woodblock, riso, data-sculpture and editorial studio looks.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features section
batches.note: Fifth production batch: first 50 remaining manually edited cards without illustrations, selected alphabetically; varied editorial styles; two brand-risk generations were discarded and replaced.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure after the target-audience section
batches.note: Manual publish illustration for Hermes Agent. One tool-specific editorial scene generated through the Codex image generation flow; no logos, readable text, or trademarked imagery.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features section
batches.note: Sixth production batch: next 10 remaining manually edited cards after the edited-tail 50 selection, using the same registry-first alphabetical selection and varied Codex image generation styles.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only technical figure after the intro section
batches.note: OCR/PDF/Rechnungsautomatisierung cluster: strict technical diagrams generated as controlled SVG-to-WebP assets, with no logos, stock photos, robots, cyberpunk, or decorative 3D.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features or practical-use section
batches.note: Seventh production batch: next 10 remaining manually edited cards after Balsamiq, generated first as PNG raster artwork and then converted to WebP. Uses varied artistic narrative editorial scenes; no SVG renderer, logos, readable text, captions, or brand marks.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features section
batches.note: Single-card continuation of the previous PNG-to-WebP tool-card illustration scheme. Uses an artistic narrative scene; no SVG renderer, logos, readable text, captions, or brand marks.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features section
batches.note: Single-card continuation of the PNG-to-WebP tool-card illustration scheme. Uses an artistic music-production and live-performance scene; no SVG renderer, logos, readable text, captions, or brand marks.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features section
batches.note: Eighth production batch: next 10 remaining manually edited cards after the previous PNG-to-WebP run, selected alphabetically from non-illustrated registry-backed tool cards. Uses varied artistic narrative editorial scenes; no SVG renderer, logos, readable text, captions, or brand marks.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features section
batches.note: Ninth production batch: next 20 remaining manually edited cards after BranchTrack, selected alphabetically from non-illustrated registry-backed tool cards. New assets are converted to 1260px WebP and the shared editorial figure display width is reduced to 90%; generated PNG originals are deleted after conversion to avoid local disk clutter. Uses varied artistic narrative editorial scenes; no logos, readable text, captions, or brand marks.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features or practical-use section
batches.note: Tenth production batch: next 50 remaining manually edited cards after Cloudera Data Platform, selected alphabetically from non-illustrated registry-backed tool cards. Assets are converted to 1260px WebP and use the shared 90% editorial figure display width. The later part of the generation run deliberately broadens settings, occupations, and visual metaphors after a user request for more varied storylines; generated PNG originals are deleted after conversion to avoid local disk clutter.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features or practical-use section
batches.note: Eleventh production batch: next 50 remaining manually edited cards after FaceApp, selected alphabetically from non-illustrated registry-backed tool cards. Styles intentionally vary across portrait studio, research greenhouse, cloud infrastructure, music studio, meeting rooms, transit systems, still life, retro poster, cosmic cockpit and other story settings. Several generated variants with extra-limb, readable-text, brand-like or screenshot artifacts were rejected and not copied into the workspace; generated originals remain in the Codex image cache per current save-path policy.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features or practical-use section
batches.note: Twelfth production batch: next 30 remaining manually edited cards after Grok, selected alphabetically from non-illustrated registry-backed tool cards. Assets were generated as raster PNG illustrations through the Codex image generation flow and converted to 1260px WebP. Two variants with visible text/flag artifacts were rejected and left only in the Codex image cache; only selected WebP outputs were copied into the workspace.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features or practical-use section
batches.note: Thirteenth production batch: next 30 remaining manually edited cards after InShot, selected alphabetically from non-illustrated registry-backed tool cards. Assets were generated as raster PNG illustrations through the Codex image generation flow and converted to 1260px WebP. Six generated variants with visible text, logo-like, platform-logo or distracting pictogram artifacts were rejected and left only in the Codex image cache; only selected WebP outputs were copied into the workspace.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features or practical-use section
batches.note: Fourteenth production batch: next 30 remaining manually edited cards after LANDR, selected alphabetically from non-illustrated registry-backed tool cards. Assets were generated as raster PNG illustrations through the Codex image generation flow and converted to 1260px WebP. Eight generated variants with figurative, visible-text, interface-like or iconographic artifacts were rejected and left only in the Codex image cache; only selected WebP outputs were copied into the workspace.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features or practical-use section
batches.note: Fifteenth production batch: next 30 remaining manually edited cards after Manus, selected alphabetically from non-illustrated registry-backed tool cards. Assets were generated as raster PNG illustrations through the Codex image generation flow and converted to 1260px WebP. Eight primary variants with figurative, readable-text, platform-logo, or profile/icon artifacts were rejected and left only in the Codex image cache; only selected WebP outputs were copied into the workspace.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features or practical-use section
batches.note: Sixteenth production batch: next 50 remaining manually edited cards after Nintex, selected alphabetically from non-illustrated registry-backed tool cards. Assets were generated as raster PNG illustrations through the Codex image generation flow and converted to 1260px WebP. Four primary variants with readable text, formula, or letter-icon artifacts were rejected; selected WebP outputs were copied into the workspace and the local PNG cache was cleaned after conversion.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features or practical-use section
batches.note: Seventeenth production batch: next 30 remaining manually edited cards after Quark, selected alphabetically from non-illustrated registry-backed tool cards. The set was regenerated after uniformity feedback with deliberately varied visual styles: paper collage, parchment map, blueprint, light isometric, studio still life, darkroom, acoustic sculpture, and pastel logistics diagrams. Selected PNG outputs were converted to 1260px WebP; variants with logo, profile/head, text, or repeated-dashboard artifacts were rejected.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features or practical-use section
batches.note: Eighteenth production-style batch: next 30 remaining manually edited cards after Rev.com, selected alphabetically from non-illustrated registry-backed tool cards, from Reverso through Scrivener. The set was regenerated after user feedback to reduce dashboard-like repetition and add story-forward scenes, including photo still lifes, paper dioramas, mixed-media collage, miniature laboratories, film sets, and physical workflow objects. Normal props such as phones, keypads, envelopes, calendars, stamps, and office pictograms were accepted when they supported the scene; selected variants still avoided readable text, logos, dominant faces/avatars, and screenshot-like dashboard compositions. Selected PNG outputs were converted to 1260px WebP, and the PNG generation cache is intended to be cleaned after conversion.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features or practical-use section
batches.note: Nineteenth production-style batch: 50 currently remaining registry-backed, non-illustrated manually edited cards, selected alphabetically from Acapela Group through Audiotool. User requested more varied illustration styles, so the set deliberately avoids dashboard-like repetition and mixes photo still lifes, paper dioramas, mixed-media collage, physical data metaphors, hardware macro scenes, audio workbenches, learning stages, and workflow objects. Generated as raster PNG through the Codex image generation flow and converted directly to 1260px WebP; no project PNG cache is kept after conversion.

batches.placement: mid-article image-only figure before the main functions/features section
batches.note: Twentieth production-style batch: next 50 registry-backed, non-illustrated manually edited cards, selected alphabetically from Automation Anywhere through DataCamp. User requested more visual variety and fewer dashboard-like images, so this batch mixes process-line miniatures, cinematic photo-collage, risograph, gouache, watercolor, paper craft, clay-style scenes, technical engraving, macro photography, and 3D dioramas. Generated as raster PNG through the Codex image generation flow and converted directly to 1260px WebP; no project PNG cache is kept after conversion.
